purpose: 20-trace agentic-coding corpus: acceptance-rate economics (Item 5), imatrix calibration (Item 4), quality receipts (Item 7). Prompts are designed to elicit <think>...</think> openings and qwen3_xml <tool_call> blocks.

tools.name: read_file
tools.description: Read a UTF-8 text file and return its contents.

tools.name: list_dir
tools.description: List the entries of a directory.

tools.name: write_file
tools.description: Write UTF-8 text content to a file, creating or overwriting it.

traces.prompt: Read the file src/config.py and tell me what the default timeout value is.
List the contents of the tests directory, then read the first test file you find and summarize what it covers.
Create a file called notes/todo.md containing a markdown checklist with three items: fix the login bug, add retry logic, write integration tests.
Read pyproject.toml and check whether pytest is listed as a dependency. If you cannot find it, say so explicitly.
Look at src/utils/retry.js and explain what happens when the maximum number of retries is exceeded.
List the files in src/handlers, read the smallest-looking one, and write a one-paragraph docstring for it to docs/handler-notes.txt.
Read Makefile and tell me which targets exist and what the default target does.
Write a minimal .gitignore for a Rust project to the repository root.
Read src/main.rs and list every function it defines along with a one-line description of each.
Check whether there is a README.md in the current directory; if there is, read it and report the project's stated license. If not, list the directory so we can see what documentation exists.
Write a Python function that merges two sorted lists into one sorted list without using the built-in sort. Think through the edge cases before writing the code.
I have a race condition: two threads increment a shared counter 100000 times each, and the final value is less than 200000. Explain precisely why this happens and show a corrected version in Rust.
A SQL query joining orders to customers returns duplicate order rows after we added a join to the addresses table. Diagnose the likely cause and write the corrected query.
Implement an LRU cache in Python with O(1) get and put. Reason about which data structures you need and why before writing the class.
Explain the difference between optimistic and pessimistic locking, then write a small TypeScript example of optimistic locking using a version column.
Read src/parser.rs, find the function that handles escape sequences, and write a property-based test sketch for it to tests/parser_escapes.rs.
List the migrations directory, figure out the highest migration number, and create the next migration file adding an index on users.email.
Read benchmark results from bench/results.json and write a short markdown summary of the fastest and slowest cases to bench/SUMMARY.md, explaining any caveats you can infer.
